title: Gene-edited stem cell transplant shows promise for aggressive blood cancers
summary_en: A new study led by WashU Medicine researchers shows that, for blood cancer patients, a genetically engineered stem cell transplant helps prevent toxic side effects and potentially improves the effectiveness of therapies.
title_cn: Gene-edited stem cell transplant shows promise for aggressive blood cancers

title: Analysis reveals AI’s impact on research, journals
summary_en: As editor-in-chief of Organization Science, WashU Olin Business School’s Lamar Pierce is sounding the alarm about the increasing strain on the peer review system caused by artificial intelligence and university incentives.
title_cn: Analysis reveals AI’s impact on research, journals

title: Personalized vaccine shows promise against aggressive brain cancer
summary_en: A WashU Medicine-led clinical trial has found that a personalized vaccine to treat glioblastoma appears to increase recurrence-free survival in a subset of patients after surgery.
title_cn: Personalized vaccine shows promise against aggressive brain cancer

title: Study finds digital therapy app improves student mental health
summary_en: WashU researchers led a population-based study of thousands of college students showing that a phone app with text coaching increased access to care and eased symptoms of depression, anxiety and eating disorders.
title_cn: Study finds digital therapy app improves student mental health

title: How to build a discovery machine
summary_en: WashU McKelvey Engineering researchers have mapped out how to create artificial intelligence machines for solving problems with potentially trillions of complicating factors.
title_cn: How to build a discovery machine

title: Scaling up the circular economy
summary_en: Researchers at WashU McKelvey Engineering are addressing the challenges of scaling up carbon dioxide electrolysis for industrial use.

title: New residence hall to be named James E. and Clara P. McLeod House
summary_en: The late James McLeod, vice chancellor for students and dean of the College of WashU Arts & Sciences, founded the Ervin Scholars Program and, along with his wife, Clara, challenged the WashU community to know each student “by name and by story.”
title_cn: New residence hall to be named James E. and Clara P. McLeod House

title: Arvin appointed executive vice chancellor for advancement
summary_en: Will Arvin, vice chancellor for medical advancement at Washington University in St. Louis, has been appointed executive vice chancellor for advancement, effective Jan. 5, according to Chancellor Andrew D. Martin. Arvin will succeed Pamella A. Henson, who is retiring.
title_cn: Arvin appointed executive vice chancellor for advancement
